analysis:
1. Bitcoin Downside Risk: Market Has Repriced Dramatically Lower

The $65k dip-by-EOY-2026 market has exploded to 99.9% (+42.45pp this week, +48.45pp this month), and the $75k dip-by-EOY-2026 market sits at 99.9% (+30.45pp this week). The $55k dip market is at 70% and the $45k dip market at 47%—both elevated significantly over the past month (+35.5pp and +26pp respectively). Current March spot pricing confirms the bear case is live: Bitcoin above $70k in March trades at 99.9% certainty, but above $75k is only 58%, above $80k is 24%, and above $85k is just 8%—placing spot Bitcoin squarely in the $70–75k range as of this briefing.

2. Upside Targets: The Bull Case Has Collapsed

The probability of Bitcoin reaching a new all-time high by December 31, 2026 has fallen to just 19%, down 2.5pp this week and 18pp this month. The $130k target is at 15% (-5.5pp this week, -17.5pp this month), $140k at 10% (-3pp this week), and $150k at 9% (-1.5pp this week). $100k by EOY 2026 sits at 41%, down 40.5pp over the past month—the single largest month-on-month collapse in the upside cluster. The market has systematically repriced the entire bull stack lower over the past 30 days.

3. March 2026 Price Window: Tight Range, Low Upside

Bitcoin reaching $150k in March is priced at 0.4%, $100k at 0.7%, $90k at 2.3%, and $85k at 8%—the near-term ceiling is firmly capped. A new all-time high by March 31 is at just 0.5%, down 0.55pp this week. The "Bitcoin hit $60k or $80k first" market sits at exactly 50%, reflecting genuine two-sided uncertainty about whether the next major level touched is higher or lower from current spot. A $65k dip in March is priced at 50.5%, and a $60k dip at 23%—meaningful crash probabilities within weeks.

4. MicroStrategy and Structural Bitcoin Catalysts

The "Nothing Ever Happens: MicroStrategy" market—betting that no major MicroStrategy event occurs by March 31—is at just 3.8% but has surged +5pp this week and an extraordinary +69pp this month, suggesting the market initially priced high drama and has since walked it back hard. Satoshi moving Bitcoin in 2026 is priced at 8.6%, barely moving (+0.6pp this week). China unbanning Bitcoin by 2027 sits at 3.9%, down 0.65pp this week. These structural/catalyst markets are all low-probability and stable, contributing no meaningful bullish momentum.

5. Key Shifts This Week

The dominant move this week is the violent repricing of the $65k dip-by-EOY-2026 market: +42.45pp to 99.9%, the largest single weekly move in the dataset. The $75k dip-by-EOY-2026 market followed with +30.45pp to 99.9%—together these represent the market fully accepting that Bitcoin will revisit these levels before year-end. On the upside, $130k by EOY lost 5.5pp to 15% and the all-time-high-by-EOY market dropped 2.5pp to 19%, continuing a month-long deterioration in bull conviction. The MicroStrategy "nothing happens" market added +5pp this week to 3.8%, consolidating a massive monthly reversal. The $25k dip-by-EOY market quietly gained +3pp to 14%—a tail-risk signal worth watching.
